mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5%, the Fed maintains a restrictive monetary stance that keeps real rates elevated. This suppresses bond demand and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield upward as investors demand compensation for holding longer-duration assets in a high-rate environment.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in more than 55% of 20-day forward periods when FEDFUNDS > 3.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained unemployment rate below 3.7% signals tight labor markets and wage-driven inflation pressure. This forces the Fed to maintain or raise rates, which increases the term premium on 10-year Treasuries and drives yields higher over the subsequent 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE remains below 3.7% for two consecutive months
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls in more than 50% of triggered instances, or correlation between UNRATE and 20-day forward yield changes is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) above trend signals robust consumer demand and economic expansion. This increases inflation expectations and forces the Fed to maintain higher rates, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield upward as the market prices in sustained monetary restriction.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS growth and 20-day forward yield changes is below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) contraction signals weakening supply-side capacity and potential stagflation risk. Markets reprice inflation expectations upward and demand higher yields on long-duration bonds, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise despite economic weakness.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls in more than 60% of triggered instances over 60-day windows, or the mechanism fails to predict yield direction with accuracy > 52% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal demand momentum. When both rise simultaneously, the market reprices inflation expectations upward and the Fed's implicit rate path higher, driving the 10-year Treasury yield up over the next 5 trading days as forward guidance adjusts.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month growth > 2% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.4%
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in more than 50% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction effect (joint signal) produces lower predictive power than either signal alone (Rank IC difference < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals wage-price spiral risk. The Fed must tighten further, and the market reprices the entire yield curve higher. The 10-year Treasury yield rises as investors demand compensation for expected sustained monetary restriction.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.2% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 3.5%
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in more than 55% of jointly-triggered instances, or the joint signal's Rank IC is not significantly higher than the univariate CPIAUCSL signal (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 3.2% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 3.0%, a policy gap emerges. The market anticipates aggressive Fed tightening and reprices long-duration bonds downward, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise sharply over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in more than 50% of triggered 60-day windows, or the policy-gap signal produces Rank IC < 0.08 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) above 2.5% annualized signals strong economic expansion that sustains inflation above target. In this regime, the 10-year Treasury yield rises as the market prices in a longer duration of elevated rates and terminal-rate expectations shift upward.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in more than 55% of 60-day forward periods when GDPC1 growth > 2.5%, or Rank IC remains below 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.
